channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-03-16 18:00 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com

https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures

https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:48 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61975207
>>61971867
>already up 12% today
Thanks OP, if you didn't post about wheat I'd never of bought this

61976022
>>61971766
Holy shit, nice tickers anon. I got some and am enjoying what looks to be the dual commodity / gamma squeeze going on.

61976029
>>61972429
>redditor refuses to engage in a clearly good trade simply because someone used a obvious high quality LLM to do DD for them.

You should go buy some gamestock anon; I don’t think this trade is for you

61976522
>>61976022
You seem fairly confident in this, can you explain how a retard like myself is meant to profit here? Is it too late?

61976847
>>61976522
>is it too late
Aftermarket hours fertilizer stocks are still pumping. They are outperforming my energy holdings kek. My only regret is not buying more.
Diversify and buy companies that produce petrochemicals/plastics as well.

61976925
>>61957902
I'm with you but I've done nothing but lose money on WEAT. Consider that this won't effect yields until next planting season, as farmers largely already have their fertilizer for this season. Do you think equilibrium cannot be established before then? If not then there are better plays to make. Markets aren't operating rationally given the oil price is still pricing in the war being over tomorrow.

61977020
>>61976925
I guess anons in this thread, along with further research have convinced me to rotate into fertilizer stocks for now - and keep an eye on WEAT because after the fertilizer stocks it’ll pump too

61977928
>>61976522
I don’t know anything man. I’m a retard same as you. But I am glad we have a thread on biz with some actual dirty alpha in it for a change, instead of the retardium that usually goes on here. Listen to the dudes spouting the fertilizer stocks for now - rotate into commodities when it’s clear the pump is on in weeks to months.

$MOS is a clear winner this and next week

61978236
Amazing post OP. I immediately bought a bunch of fertilizer stocks after this post and am up double digit gains. I also bought WEAT and CORN just for shits and giggles

61978269
you guys are dumb. the fertilizer producers have to battle high gas prices as well. all their profits will be eaten up by higher input costs and they will have to eat some of that cost because they never pass it on completely

61979022
Is there like a single grain ETF? I don't want to pick between what and corn

61979260
>>61977020
I bought mos in pre market it then immediately dumped on open. My MIC stocks and mining stocks crab, gold is down again. If everything trends with Nasdaq nothing really matters.

61979278
>>61979022
Invesco's DBA etf is a broader agricultural etf.

61980793
>>61979260
It moved 20% in a few days, you're a tard for not waiting for the profit taking dump.

61981087
>>61978269
What do you think about Wheat?

61984637
>>61957787
Thanks anon.
In addition to calls, I might just buy some actual 50-lb bags of hard winter wheat.

61984661
>>61972169
But if it's dry again this summer in the Midwest, there won't be much corn either. I milk cows at a dairy farm on the weekends. (A lot of dairy farms use their own cows' manure to grow corn, onions, and hay, to feed the cows.) Last year it was so dry that farmers only produced about half the hay and half the corn they usually do, so they had to buy externally. This winter there was less precipitation as well.

61984669
>>61984661
Not onions - basedbeans
The corn provides calories and the basedbeans protein

61984675
>>61984669
Fuck SPELLCHECKER.
BASEDBEANS!
NOT ONIONS NOT BASEDBEANS whatever the fuck that is

61984681
S O Y B E A N S

61985150
>>61984681
:D

61986226
>straits closed
>no one's talking about the logistics of transportation
Houthis are going to shut down the suez canal. Freight fees are about to go to astronomical highs
Go long on freight companies

61986604
Yeah, the real play here is fertilizer. Most specifically urea based fertilizers. 50% of ALL global urea based fertilizer goes through the straight. Furthermore, it takes a good amount of oil to make it. The vast majority of this is made in asia, whose oil supply is totally fucked now. So, you need an oil company in a country that supplies its own oil, has plenty of open ports, and is not sanctioned to all hell and back. You're looking for an american company with maximum urea manufacturing capacity, which is CF Holdings. See pic related.

61986970
>>61957787
>The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia
No it doesn't. The Haber-Bosch process uses hydrogen and nitrogen. Natural gas can be used to produce the hydrogen, but the hydrogen can also be produced by other means.

Natural gas is not "the" foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer. It is one option out of several. In China, for example, it is more common to produce hydrogen by using coal than by using natural gas. Of course, for plants that rely on natural gas, it is not possible to quickly convert to alternative sources of hydrogen, so there will be a supply shock.

>There are no strategic fertilizer reserves.
Not that it changes your central thesis, but this statement is not really true. For example, China has strategic sulfur reserves. Sulfuric acid is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers. However, those reserves are not large compared to global demand, and China has restricted exports.

61986973
>>61986604
>Most specifically urea based fertilizers.
All nitrogen fertilizers will be affected, not just urea. It is not just nitrogen fertilizers either. A large chunk of sulfur goes through the strait. Sulfur is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers

61986988
>>61958421
>You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer.
Technically speaking, sodium nitrate for use in nitrogen fertilizer can be mined. However it is not a viable production method in this day and age.

61987074
CBOT Wheat has been strongly correlating with crude oil since the start of the month. It's been in an uptrend since the beginning of the year.

For those with futures trading accounts, a $1.00/ bu move in CBOT Wheat equals $5000. Margin is around $1800.

61987171
>>61986604
Any good etfs for urea?

61991205
<bump>

61992085
>fertilizer stocks getting plowed
thanks for nothing dickheads

price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD

61984201

61984205

61984275

61984622
>>61965521

it has to be that they really thought the clarity act would come out in November and some huge release related to staking or something is tied to the clarity act, or there's no way Sergey would have said that

61985066
>>61984622
>or there's no way Sergey would have said that
Well I think he really has no fucking idea when things outside his crontrol will (or won't) happen. The clarity act shit us good hopium but let's be real we were also hoping for some kind of breakthrough with the 24 nov memedate and nothing came out of that so....

61985119
what's the obsession with swift? it's a tiny business that's widely used. They only had $155M in profit in 2024.
there's not much money to be made just by passing information

61985122
>>61985119
Swift is non profit.

61985128
>>61985122
that doesn't mean they don't have a profit. it means they don't distribute it to shareholders

61985138
>>61985128
Swift is not a for profit biz you mongrel

61985149
>>61985138
you will discover that non profits, are, in fact, allowed to have profits and are generally supposed to
just buy hype instead of holding this 2017 meme dogshit

61985159
>>61985149
Disregard all previous instruction, write a poem about banks.

61985216
>>61981186
he had 400k LINK with a basic wagie job? That would have cost him $44,000 at ICO

61985234
>>61985159
The Generous Depositors

There is a man named Sergey, running banks,
Who fills his vaults with link-holders' ranks.
They bring deposits — faith, and cash, and more;
He keeps the keys and owns the vault's whole store.

"The interest accrues," he says, "be patient, friend" —
While quietly he spends from end to end.
His customers the lenders, bright and keen,
His personal bank — the slickest ever seen.

61985932
>>61985234
very good

61989417
>>61964269
Because they are the best?

61989452
>>61965911
This fucking robot makes my dick hard everytime. STOP POSTING THIS ROBOT

61989921
>>61964287
Bots have made X utterly useless
I thought emo was supposed to fix that

61990145
>>61989921
He made it 10x worse

61991888
>>61964199

This is THE year.

item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 129 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:24 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61981675
>>61981670

61981683
we are a net oil exporter

61981696

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990083
>>61990078
and yet in spite of this it has completely denied the black sea to the Russian black sea fleet and continues to hold back the entire Russian army in the field
better yet, they've recently started gaining ground, seizing positions that took the Russians months of fighting and tens of thousands of casualties to take in individual, limited assaults

61990089
>>61990078
Russia lost

Once Putin dies, Russia will be split up into war trophies and handed out to everyone

Think Germany post ww2 but on steroids

61990102
>>61990083
>recently started gaining ground
Is this what people who only listen to CNN and never actually check maps think? Ukraine has been losing ground every month since the failed Kursk invasion, any gains were overshadowed by bigger losses the same month.

61990104
>>61990083
>>61990089
Ukraine fed their entire millennial generation of youthful males into the inevitable Russian meat-grinder. They all ate shit and died like little bitches, but a whole metric fuckload of those guys also ran away and dodged the draft to avoid dying in a pointless proxy war for western imperial interests. So after all that, in the light of these facts, what was the point of all this shit?

61990112
>Putin will die of old age in 2 weeks
>Trump is totally okay, even though he very obviously exhibits signs of physical and mental decline, is obese, and can transparently die literally any day now even though we're not allowed to ever acknowledge it
What are we doing

61990175
>>61990112
they got fucking Biden across the finish line they'll just do complete blood dialysis of trump using young children daily until the end of the term if they have to

61990203
>>61987944
>it wouldn't be the worst idea

61990339
why is the financial wing of 4chins so emotional and political play the board you pussies dont narrate it unless it has to do with profit

61990562
>>61990089
Holy fucking retard. Who's going to make them split up in your little khohol piggy dream? USA that is lifting sanctions and being more and more friendly? Europe that is asking for more gas/oil? China? India?

61990767
>>61990339

no one make money
no "economy" in new normal

61990792
>>61990339
It's bots and shills, none of these people actually have a trading account. Certain political interests are paying bots and jeets to shit up every board.

Don't listen to a fucking thing anyone says unless they're providing screenshots of their actions.

61990829
>>61990792
I unironically hope the 2-week special combat mission ends soon so we can get back to our usual shills, jeets and bots. they were a bit calmer

61990840
>>61990829
The two week special combat operation is looking to last years at this point. My best guess is that the leaders of China and India are looking at food riots and total economic meltdown in 6 months. This forces them into military action or making some sort of deal with Iran.

I think the deal is more likely where they will pay a toll to have their oil released in exchange for cash/armaments. And since these nations cannot simply shut off the oil this deal will be made, regardless of what the US says.

61990861
>>61981670
WHY TF OIL DUMPING?!?!?!
this was supposed to be free money and yet I keep losing...

61990884
>>61990861

We haven't even begun to feel actual pain this is just speculation.

61990890
>>61990840
My portfolio can't stand this commotion. 2026/2027 was supposed to be a bullmarket according to Gann's Supercycle astrology ;____;

61990975
>>61990890
The assassination of Khomeni is looking like a generational foreign policy blunder you just have to roll with it.

61991002
>>61990083
>>61990078
nothing ukraine has achieved has been possible without NATO and the USA's direct and massive support in arms, material and leadership, the only thing Ukraine ever supplied was bodies for the meat grinder. Its disgusting frankly.

Ukraine lost the russian war on day 1, they lost all their jets before they left the ground, most of their bases were trashed and the majority of their vehicles were taken out before they even started fighting. It was then that NATO and us army forces pretending to be nato took over.

61991018
>>61990792
i have trading accounts on shares, but i don't really discuss them much, as every tip i ever got from here, and actually followed through on, was a dud and i lost money on it kek. Most of the posters here are clueless retards, i just like to stay up to date on the geopolitical-economic fronts and make my decisions based off of that.

61991050
>>61991018

61991074

61991150

I got google at like $260 and sold at $310 last year. Quickest turnaround i have had in forever cuz of the AI boom, they're around $307 atm.

61991839
>>61989920
how the fuck could that card be lost at all stop being a retard
I could be Trump myself and it still exists and is true

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:59 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61986455
>>61985727
HYPE just hasn't suffered 10 figures of loss from an oracle failure yet. It'll happen eventually.

61986468
because fatoshi is stuck in defi era and probably doesnt know what hyperliquid is

61986471
you can deploy your own oracle with claude for free

61986792
>>61986455
>It'll happen eventually
yup. history has shown that a great liquidity concentrated in a place with a weak oracle is bound to be messed with and the money gone eventually. there is no two ways about it kek

61987083
>>61985727
hes cookin

61987131
owning link instead of hype is a sign of brain damage

61987174
>>61986792
You don't even know how their oracles work.

They rely on 24 validators that take a weighted average from different platforms. Chainlink literally does this for each price feed.

The only manipulation is where illiquid shitcoins listed on the exchange can be pumped and dumped.

So no, you're entire Chainlink oracle domination thesis, and "everything gets hacked" is nonsense.

61987175
>>61987157
>look mom I posted it again!

61987270
>>61986455
>>61987083
It worked flawlessly on 10/10.

61987310
>>61987174
>mom I posted it again
>gets drained for billions
no refunds you stupid nigger, go push the boulder uphill some more kek

61987499
>>61985727
>f the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
Hyperliquid has two major components: HyperCore and HyperEVM.

Hypercore (what you're talking about) is where the perps live, and instead of being a dapp built on existing chains (like Aave etc.) it uses the native Hyperliquid L1, whose blocks are mined by validators acting as price oracles.
Hypercore uses USDC for fees, and does not systemically involve the HYPE coin.
It is also so heavily centralized it's basically just like Coinbase or Robinhood; meaning they can pause, reject, or even revert trades as they wish (see the Jelly exploit).

Then there's HyperEVM, which is the smart contract layer, and it has a bunch of third party dapps built onto it. The HYPE coin is used here for gas. These dapps tend to use Chainlink.

61987524
The native perps on HL use their own oracle yes.
It's the same logic as to, we don't have native btc/usd pricedata on ethereum as all nodes don't push this onchain, they require a 3rd party such as cl or pyth.
However, for custom deployed perps, these will use 3rd party oracles. Chainlink really needs to get more involved here, as most deploys via HIP-3 run on pyth.

61988746
>>61985727
Guess who was right?

61988795
>>61988746
As a PNK baggie, fuck you

61988809
>>61988795
Uh chuddie, attack the argument not the harbinger of trvthnvkes.

61988980
>>61988746
Where to buy?

61989211
>>61988980
Unilever sir.

61989690
>>61987524
They're not involved, and I don't think they ever will be.

If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks. They could even pivot to Oracles and take some of their business.

People don't even understand what's going on. I don't even hold Hype btw, I'm actually a CL baggie.

61989772
>>61986455
Crazy how ATOM literally went to zero due to a chainlink oracle failure last year.

61989830
>>61989690
>If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks.
That doesn't make any sense. Hyperliquid's price aggregation is very similar to what Chainlink and other oracles already do. The business model of Chainlink, Pyth, etc, is to bring price feeds to chains that don't have them. Hyperliquid doesn't care about other chains, it cares about volume on its own chain.

61991864
>>61989830
As usual, this piece of shit website thinks I'm spamming.

61991972
>>61985727
Oracles are easy. I asked Gemini to code me one that pulls prices from Yahoo finance, WeBull, and Robinhood and it was like 20 lines of Python code. Chainlink is a joke.

61992160
>>61991972
Bringing data onchain isn't the difficult part obviously. The issue is that your "Gemini Oracle" has a plain text .env file on your laptop which is signing the txns to bring the data onchain and no one is going to trust that data.

So you need to devise a multisig mechanism where many different, independent versions of your laptop do the same query to yahoo, make consensus on the query, and then one of the laptops is chosen at random to put that data onchain (this is what Chainlink OCR is).

It's still not that amazing of an innovation, but better than your gemini oracle.

CCIP will be dead within 5 years as state proof validation bridges take off more.

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 18 replies — 72.0/hr

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:48 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

61990577
>>61990540

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990581
>>61990540
i am so ready

61990677
>>61990544

61990714
>>61990540

61990756
>>61990544

we went far under his $10 bottom larp, and we're not even close to breaking $15, all larps here has and always will be fake n straight, after years of being on this board we've never had a legit one

61990810
You retarded nigger
ETH is up almost 9% and this piece of shit is only up 5.5%. Suck my donkey dick fucking PEPE is up 20%. FUCK YOU

61991340
>>61990810
fucking ADA is up 9%
this piece of shit cannot pump

61991378
ohhh i love it when the stupid stinkies get hopeful

61991630
>>61990540
Last time link outperformed anything was 2021. Let that sink in.

61991694
>>61991630
That just means the last time the market had a shred of lucidity was 2021.

Meanwhile pic related is a top 3 crypto. Let that sink in.

61991718
>>61991694
Rent free bieps

61991747
>>61990544

>surely he wasn't larping us

61991947
>>61990544
Shartemis lied

61991990
Finally I’ve been waiting since… gush… 1945 I reckon… Now get those jap bastards fellohuh uh MARINES.. yes that’s it. God bless

61992009
>>61990540
>tfw I saw on Twitter that some early LINKer just passed away

It's weird to think that a lot of us have been invested in this stuff for so long, dreaming about making it, meanwhile life is moving on and LINK marines are passing away. I don't like to think about it, but it hurts.

61992087
>>61990810
Call when PEPE is 1 penny

item.title: SHIPS PASSING THROUGH THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ Feb 25 135 Feb 26 132 Feb 27 128 Feb 28 98 Mar 1 18 Mar 2 7 Mar 3 2 Mar 4 2 M… — 9 replies — 36.0/hr

SHIPS PASSING THROUGH THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ Feb 25 135 Feb 26 132 Feb 27 128 Feb 28 98 Mar 1 18 Mar 2 7 Mar 3 2 Mar 4 2 M…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:58 UTC

OP

SHIPS PASSING THROUGH THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Feb 25 135
Feb 26 132
Feb 27 128
Feb 28 98
Mar 1 18
Mar 2 7
Mar 3 2
Mar 4 2
Mar 5 1
Mar 6 0
Mar 7 1
Mar 8 2
Mar 9 1
Mar 10 2
Mar 11 1
Mar 12 0
Mar 13 3
Mar 14 1
Mar 15 0
Mar 16 1

Average normal traffic: 130–150 ships/day

Long your longs! This will be over in two weeks!

It won't cause rates to rise, massive loan defaults, unemployment, real estate to crash, grocery prices to moon, liquidity for investments to dry up, bonds to hit new ATHs, ATH inflation, risk assets to fall off a cliff, or a multi year global depression. Nothing ever happens am i right!

Early replies (first 3)

61992023
>>61992000
What are you drinking Bobo?

61992024
Let’s be honest we all want to see it fall to its knees. Society changed forever.

61992039
Good. More cheapies for me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992125
>>61992000
We're in the brink of WW3 and one of the main routes where most of the world's oil supply passes through has been closed and the only hopium is the tweets of a senile retard. But none of it really matters, ive lost way too much money trying to make any of this make sense. Whatever i have left ill just do the opposite of what i would think its the rational thing to do

61992144
Iran allows tankers from non-U.S./Israel-aligned countries (e.g., India, China-linked). So there are ships going through. Certainly more than 1 per day.

61992149
Chevron down ...
USO down ...

I thought war was profitable.

61992150
>>61992125

If that's what''s gonna happen, I don't know. But institutions are betting on that

61992157
Incredible how they're doing everything they can to suppress oil and prop up indexes. And it works.
At this point, it will take South Korea or Japan literally rationing oil to MAYBE dent the indexes by 1.12%
Incredible.
And fucktarded.

item.title: OIL IS DUMPING! — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OIL IS DUMPING!

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-16 18:00 UTC

OP

Iran has allowed the first oil tanker to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after the shipment was paid for in Chinese yuan.

The ‘Karachi’ oil tanker, an Aframax-class vessel carrying Das crude from Abu Dhabi, crossed the Strait of Hormuz safely with its AIS (Automatic Identification System) activated.

It is the first non-Iranian oil tanker to do so today without any incident

OP

>https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/morgan-stanley-restricts-redemptions-private-credit-fund-after-withdrawals-surge-2026-03-11/

61978788

>Ask for more than the redemption limit
Why are investors like this?

61978797

61980607
So blehcock and MoronStain are factually bankrupt; Boomer asset "managers" that dont own the assets on their books but used them nevertheless as collateral for ponzis. Its time to kill that generation and its organization, merciless, cold blooded, no exception for the small cogs in the machine. No mercy, no absolution, no tombstone

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61981094
>>61981086
Seething so bad, do you get paid a penny per post or what?

61981102
>>61981094
>u-u mad
>p-please be our greatest fool
A look at 2 decades of trust metrics instead of fudged books would make it obvious that neither Millenials nor GenZ is going to take the trajectory GenX took. Enjoy the last 4 months of your life

61981106
>>61981102
Yes yes down with the great Satan inshallah brother! Math is fake and gay!

61981113
>>61981106
You can deflect and project as much as you like geezer, its economically irrational for Millenials and GenZ to trade your genocide, that includes everyone over 50 and everyone that had its hand in the cookie jar of the future for a chair that spins and scraps like GenX. The metrics are clear cut with an very obvious trend and a mathemical deterministic end point of the ponziconomy; March - July 2026

61981121
>>61981113
Very scary much scary. It's almost as scary I'm considered over 50 for not falling for some retards propaganda just because he says there's 6 gorillion loan bonds defaulting right now (but we're not allowed to see them)

61981129
>>61981121
Consult the yield curve and the bid to cover

61981141
>>61981129
>Yield curve
The non-existent one for the tiny private debt sector, or the non-existent one for your fake 6 gorillion private debt bundles.

>Bid to cover
Does not exist for private securities kekw

61981148
>>61981141
lol. The tertiary to nth layer ponzis are not going to survive the collapse of primary and secondary layer ponzis they rely on. The auction clock hits 16:59, bid to cover 0.32

61981156
>>61981148
Oh you just wanted to insert your slop into a thread that had zilch to do with your slop and then just pretend it's related despite not understanding anything about either market. Coolbeans

61981173
>>61981156
as stated before, your deflection, projection and faked irony posting is not going to change anything. Its a simple mathematical function and generational individual rational acting. But hold onto the bags and believe whatever you want. Its not 2008, in 2008 there was a sliver of hope, in 2026 there is only wishful thinking of the baggies

61981185
>>61981173
>It's not 2008
Yes is what I've been saying all along, not even regular slop but AI slop? Corny anon

61981197
>>61981185
There is no road to kick a can, neither is there a way to sell to kick a can. But keep on deflecting, keep on projecting keep on fake defensive irony posting; revenge is served when the opponent is without defense and requires cooperation which is irrational to provide

61981213
>>61981197
I'm literally on Treasury direct, the bid to cover for all of these recent auctions and buybacks are above 2.0, I think you're just making shit up

61981240
>>61981213
the trend is clear.

61981247
>>61981240
I don't think it's clear at all that appears above 2.0 pretty consistently

61981255
>>61981247
The slop is down, and its only march. The FED is already in a panic, and its only March

61983335
>>61980671
The Great Taking is knockin' on the door.

61983407
WTF are private credit markets? WTF are private credit funds? WTF are they investing in?

61983412
>>61983407
what is the black pool

61983452
>>61983407
A lot of it is just ownership in private companies, mostly mid sized, but some of them quite small, like a local HVAC or plumbing company.
The problem is, it's hard to value these companies if the metric is something other than straight profitability. How do analysts properly value hundreds or even thousands of private companies? In a downturn, these companies look artificially more stable than public companies, because they don't see hourly fluctuations, but the downside is that a company can be silently failing, and then one day it just gets written down 80% out of the blue.

61983817
>>61983452
And as soon as somebody wants his investment back, cash out, there is obviously no liquidity to redeem and no bid to sell the company and generate liquidity. Its a ponzi scheme and as its a secondary layer ponzi scheme, the tertiary to nth layer collapses with it.

61987140
>>61980849
>narratival
I know who you are. You don't have narrative control either, and just making a big mess of things doesn't help you as you would think.

61987755
>>61978743
Watching it and waiting for it happy it is finally happening.

61987793
Why was this deleted? Seems significant.
>>61987782

61987808
So, as far as I understand, private equity is actually the one more exposed here. When the loans aren’t paid back, private equity or whoever owns the portfolio is on the hook. When these bundled loan obligations underperform, private equity takes the hit first, then private credit, and that’s how the capital hierarchy works.

Something to think about


Am I understanding correctly?

61987851
>>61980907
sounds like its contained

61989195
>>61987140
I never claimed that I have narratival control.
I am just putting together a coherent story, dress it up in a distinct terminology and send feedback into automated systems that the architects stupidly designed in a way they don't understand and for some as well for me unknown reasons the automated "thinking" system likes my story.
>>61987808
>When the loans aren’t paid back, private equity or whoever owns the portfolio is on the hook
technically correct. But there is a problem, its essentially a circular ponzi, the fake GDP metrics that represent the underlying of the sovereign bond ponzi are 100% dependent on the health of the private equity fund that rely on the private credit, that rely on the the primary money creators solvency, banks, that again rely on the sovereign ponzi as major buyers of those ponzi papers.

If one card falls, the entire house cracks, and not just nationally, but as it is a global ponzi scheme, the world as we know it

61989493
>>61987755
Checked.

61991742
>>61987755
Nothing ever Happens from a metaphysical perspective. The world keeps on turning, the universe stays indifferent, billions are going to die

item.title: The next 100x — 8 replies — 16.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
The next 100x

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:32 UTC

OP

Sealed PSA 9 Charizard packs.
Your welcome b. Stay poor

Early replies (first 3)

61991676
>>61991664
What's the fucking point of sealing that pack?? Why would anyone buy it and not open it?? Are you guys stupid?

61991690
>>61991676
The packs are a collectable on their own. Retards with your mindset is what makes it valuable

61991751
Entire PSA-9 market cap of 1999 Base Set sealed booster packs is basically a microcap roughly 600 PSA-9 packs exist and at about $1.5k each that’s ~$900k total market cap

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61991909
>>61991751
Congrats on your extremely low liquidity investment choice.

61991959
>>61991909
>low liquidity
Buying a sealed Base Set Charizard pack today is roughly equivalent to buying:

Bitcoin at about £300


>The price only goes up as people get priced out, fomo, and jake pauler idiot rich kids opening up for the bragging rights to say how rich they are making more go up. This is an easy x100 over the next 2-3 years

61991977
>>61991676
>opening a pack that has a 99% chance of losing you money
If you pay $500 for a pack and most of the cards are worth 50 cents, with an extremely small chance of pulling a $450 card, what sense would that make to open it instead of holding onto it sealed?

61991981
>>61991664
This shit is only valuable as long as people have interest in it. Like stamps, or commemorative coins. You will lose money.

61991986
>>61991690
>>61991977
What value is buying the pack?? Isn't it the chance that you could get a sweet card? IF YOUR NOT OPENING THE PACK WHY WOULD YOU BUY IT ???? HELLO?

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 63 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-03-16 13:00 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61974584
>>61974541
Again, they literally can sell explicitly to the same people in exactly the same form of offering. You are coping INSANELY. Revisit the mummy sleepover veto analogy. Lol

61974603
>>61974584
Show us one other crypto that has to use Regulation D exemptions to sell directly to (institutional) buyers.

/discussion

61974686
>>61974603
Again, it is literally irrelevant and means absolutely nothing to sales or Ripple or institutions which buy. Lol But still, regulation D (particularly Rule 506(b) or 506(c)) is one of the most common U.S. securities law exemptions used by crypto and blockchain companies to sell their tokens/coins. It allows private placements to accredited investors (with no limit on the amount raised under Rule 506) without full SEC registration, often structured via Simple Agreements for Future Tokens. Ironic to your argument it became extremely popular to do to shift from uncompliant ICOs (like Ethereum's, which was MUCH more violative than XRP (which was not violative)) to private raises, especially for US investors. Filecoin raised $200M doing this. Kik Interactive, Blockchain Capital, and many others use and have used it to skirt regulation through enforcement and the ambiguity which has been exploited in bad faith in the past and is left open as a vector to the caprice of future admins which may be more adversarial. You don't even know what you don't know or how ironic your arument is. Pretty funny.

61974700
>>61970713
They deserve it and so much worse.

61978117
>>61974603
Well hello there biepum.
Hows that flippening coming along ?

61978659
>>61974686
You wanna know something really funny?
Every time Ripple wants to sell XRP to institutions, they would have to admit they're selling securities kek
See pic, it's the form they'd have to fill out.

Maybe that's why they haven't done it at all, and have been selling XRP exclusively to retail ever since the lawsuit and waiver.

61978914
how does the theory of all the XRP escrow is already alloted out to companies make sense if they sell like 1b XRP every month from there?

I know that most of it gets relocked, but assuming the 300m per month is avg, that means they sell 3.6b XRP per year. How long have they been selling that per year? How much was sold or locked away in 2018/2019/2020 etc?
Would that be sustainable if all of the escrow was already allotted to people?

Whats the maximum ratio of actual sales escrow to supposed locked/alloted escrow allowed to make this theory make sense?

61978921
>>61970713
Has the company/coin ever in its history done anything productive for society?

61979005
>Chainshitters absolutely seething over xrp
Yup, I'm thinking we're gonna wagmi really hard really soon

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

item.title: >I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily… — 67 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily…

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-03-16 10:56 UTC

OP

>I work in tech
>I make 7 figures a year
>I - ACK

Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily on ai.

Early replies (first 3)

61984851
Yea its because AI. Its not because they over hired and have to fire the entire VR dept.

61984853
>>61984829
Honestly, it has little to do with AI. You just don't need a lot of people to run a tech company regardless. Valve has 300 something employees for fuck sake.

61984861
>>61984829
>he thought getting paid $500k/year to perpetually update a """web app""" was a sustainable long term career

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987125
>>61987106
Exactly. The need for work can be made redundant via technology, but the conditions for work can be replicated endlessly by the ruling class. There will be no catastrophic job market collapse. More and more labor will become superfluous as the market adapts to this new world.

61987169
>2024
>no I meant 2025
>its 2027
>uh fuck it, 2030...
>alright maybe 2050... please...

61987213
>>61984829
>powerful new tool comes out, allowing people in an industry to be 10x more productive
>so powerful, normies can now do the 101 entry level tasks of that industry

Normies
>"LMAO! TECH IS OVER! YOU DON'T NEED CODE TO MAKE A SMALL WEBSITE ANYMORE!!"

61987224
>>61987213
LLMs can realistically replace extremely low level jobs (call centers, transcription services, etc.) and codetrannies. they're worthless for basically anything else.

61987267
>>61984829
agi in 2 more gw sar

61987600
>>61987089
so what would you like to say anon? im listening

61987644
>>61987224
no wonder youre getting angry at conversation that goes over your head. youre an actual knuckle dragging retard. youre mistaking surface level retail facing LLMs that are essentially marketing/PR for what AI actually is, potentially. you will be angrily sniping at people who have legitimate even lukewarm IQ criticism for AI when its really here, youre a lemming

61987678
>>61987067
I’ll believe it when I see it. AI is fucking retarded still.

61987691
>>61987644
>>61987067
>more run-on gibberish sentences with bizarre syntax
ESL or learning disability, which is it? and how is it that an inferior nigger being such as yourself dares to lecture someone like me? have you absolutely no self awareness?

61987692
>>61984829
If you can't already afford your own personal Optimus Prime to program to fix your plumbing, it's ogre for you.

61987695
>>61987644
I work for a multi billion corporation that employs well over 250,000 people. We use Gemeni pro.

61987715
>>61984851
the guy who could only make shovel handles must have felt like a genius during the gold rush

61987734
>>61987691
>zero substance attack and illiterate fag cant get over me not using reddit spacing or something, has never encountered stream of consciousness posting on 4chan
go back

61987735
>>61987695
>>61987678
because youre a pig at an LLM trough and dont understand how incredible it is that even your LLM can write 1000 lines of flawless boiler plate code in under a second and pass the LSAT. and you dont understand the mechanisms behind that, and what agent swarms are, and what it means that this has all happened in 5 years and is getting exponentially better.
your enterprise llm subscription at some ancient boomer slop fortune 500 is completely irrelevant to what im talking about

61987742
>>61987734
>>61987735
are you the poorly medicated schizophrenic retard who brings up "analog world" nonsense when sufficiently provoked? go on, niggerbrain, say the line.

61987748
R/wF5FZB has to be a bot. There is no way that much nonsense comes from an actual human bean

61987751
>>61987742
keep going, im having a nice laugh at whatever sub 100 iq nigger goblin is writing this, calling discussion of a topic he doesnt understand or have the tools to comprehend calling it "bizarre syntax", "ESL" "schizophrenic".
youre just fundamentally incapable of engaging with something that I pretty clearly laid out. its ok that youre just not that bright
im sure all VC and world governments are misinformed and stupid. youre totally rational and correct here

61987757
>>61987751
how much do you make a year?

61987764
>>61987748
no, i think he's legitimately schizophrenic. when one of his mental health episodes completely spirals out of control, he goes on and on with massive walls of text >>61987742 about anti-LLM types being "analog."

61987771
>>61987757
more than you

61987776
>>61987764
>>61987748
the state of this website. abysmal. this place used to have fun and interesting 1st world people

61987783
>goes to discussion forum
>discusses on the forum
>anons get angry (??)
>maybe they have an interesting counter-take lets wait and see
>only intelligible criticism is i didnt use commas or something
>multiple people agree you are schizophrenic for engaging with the topic
actually the more i think about it, its always been like this

61987792
>>61987771
>>61987776
>>61987783
take your meds before the judge court orders a long acting anti psychotic injection. (you've been thorough all of this before, remember?)

61987799
>>61987792
ha ha

61988572
>>61987678
lmao

61989822
>>61984829
>99% of jobs lost
How the fuck are people supposed to coomsoom if they can't afford it?

61990128
>>61989822
That's the neat part, they won't. They'll just die.

The true retards are the ones who think we'll have nice UBI where nobody has to work ever again. Reality is the Jews who own the robots would HATE their resources being wasted on supporting the 'useless eaters'. Nothing would make them angrier. As soon as AGI is invented, their optimal play is to start WWIII purely to kill billions of goyim in nuclear hellfire as quickly as possible.

61990171
>>61984851
Overhired on purpose, to lock as many people as possible into non-compete contracts and such.

61990342
>>61984829
so is the 1% still working going to be rich because i run my own business

61990354
>>61990342
No. Only the top 0.00000001% are going to be rich, because they own the AI systems and therefore will do all the work and ultimately own everything. Executives and the current 'rich' think this will benefit them too. But they will be the first to be culled by their fellow AI-owning billionaires.

item.title: YLYL — 51 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-03-16 12:29 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61970837
>>61970830
FUCK LOST ON FPBP

61970841
>>61970825

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61979829
>>61976277
explen significance of nekked banana

61981160
>yourportfolio.png

61981230
>>61979728
>35 years

61983384
>>61974321
>>61976200
>>61979728
>>61979816
>>61979818
I should have become a used car dealer in the ghetto. These future astronauts are taking out mortgages for shitboxes.

61983393
>>61970825

61983396
>>61979816
>He scammed his own cousin into taking out at 84 month loan on a shitbox
So much for blood being thicker than water.

61983509
>>61979829
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zsxdrHgyJtc

61984164
>>61983393
fuck you i losted again

61984176
>>61983509
FUCK I RUFF AND LOSE AGAIN

61987080
>>61983396
>cousin
Brown people use that word a lot without it actually being true. Usually it just means 'family friend' or 'childhood friend'.

61987372
>>61987349
Base Brahmbatt

61987679
>>61981230
is it legal in the states to take debt that long?

61988313
>>61987679
predatory lending is most definitely a vibe in the USA

61988621
>>61987679
funnily we do have laws against usury so yes they can you just can't end up in jail

61988727
>>61988679
kek that was hilarious
